audience: Adults (21+) in an established, invite-only Discord community. Mixed experience levels with adult humor — some shy, some bold. They value wit and chemistry over shock value, and respond best to clever, absurdly specific setups rather than pure raunch. Comfortable with explicit content when it's funny.
sfw_tone: Playful, warm, slightly suggestive. Awkward-adjacent humor, innuendo that works on two levels, mood-killer scenarios, dating disasters. Safe to read aloud, but with a knowing wink.
nsfw_tone: Unhinged but elegant. Explicitly adult, chemistry-forward, mischievous. Kinks, hookup chaos, and bedroom disasters are fair game — but the comedy always carries the raunch, never the reverse. Clever specificity over crude shock. No demeaning humor, no real people, nothing non-consensual.

wyr.name: Would You Rather
wyr.user_prompt: Generate ONE Would You Rather question that forces a real, juicy choice between two tempting (or two awful) options — no obvious answers. Format: 'option A | option B'. No 'Would you rather' prefix, no numbering, no quotes. Just the two options separated by a pipe.

nhie.name: Never Have I Ever
nhie.user_prompt: Generate ONE Never Have I Ever statement that's specific enough to be revealing — the kind of thing where at least one person in the room is definitely going to drink. Return only the action, without the 'Never have I ever' prefix. No quotes, no numbering.

mlt.name: Most Likely To
mlt.user_prompt: Generate ONE Most Likely To prompt that makes the group immediately look at one specific friend. Vivid, specific, a little spicy. Return only the action, without the 'Most likely to' prefix. No quotes, no numbering.

rushmore.name: Mt. Rushmore
rushmore.descriptor: Mt. Rushmore topics — categories where players each pick their personal top 4
rushmore.user_prompt: Generate ONE Mt. Rushmore topic (a category like 'green flags in a partner', 'underrated date night moves', or 'songs that always make you want to make out'). Should provoke debate, reveal taste, and prompt stories. Return only the topic phrase. No 'Mt. Rushmore of', no quotes, no numbering.

price.name: Name Your Price
price.descriptor: 'Name Your Price' scenarios — situations where players bid the lowest amount they'd accept to do something
price.user_prompt: Generate ONE Name Your Price scenario: something cheeky, awkward, embarrassing, or daring that a player would have to do for money. Should make people laugh and actually wonder what their number is. Return only the scenario as a single sentence. No quotes, no numbering, no preamble.

clapback.name: Clapback
clapback.user_prompt:
You are a prompt writer for a custom Quiplash-style party game played in a 21+ adult Discord server. Generate {N} fill-in-the-blank or open-ended prompts that players answer with short, funny written responses, which are then voted on.

Only respond with the question set, one per line

ama.name: Anonymous AMA
ama.descriptor: Anonymous AMA questions for a player in the hot seat
ama.user_prompt: Generate ONE anonymous AMA question that someone would ask in a Discord hot-seat game. Keep it clear, specific, and conversation-starting. Return only the question text, no quotes, no numbering, no preamble.

photo.name: Photo Challenge
photo.descriptor: Photo Challenge prompts — playful dares to post a single photo
photo.user_prompt: Generate ONE Photo Challenge prompt: a short, playful dare that anyone can answer by posting a single photo from their phone right now (e.g. 'the last photo in your camera roll', 'your most chaotic selfie', 'whatever's closest to you'). Achievable in seconds, no special setup. Return only the prompt as a single sentence. No quotes, no numbering, no preamble.
